claimed_boundary: packages/opencode/src/storage/ pool lifecycle — creation, config, close, error handling, retry, degradation
consumer_purpose: Understand how PG connections are created, configured, closed, and error-handled across all storage modules; identify gaps for reliability hardening

(1)_pool_creation_sites.context: Primary PG Pool creation. This is the pg module's Pool (from 'pg' import at line 2). Only created when connectionString is NOT :memory:/file:/path — i.e. a real postgres:// URL. The PGlite branch (line 15) uses new PGlite(dataDir) instead, which is an in-process WASM-based embedded PG.
(1)_pool_creation_sites.duality: PGlite at line 15 vs real Pool at line 18 — the init() function is a routing function, not a pure pool constructor

(1)_pool_creation_sites.code: const pool = new DuckDBConnectionPool(resolvedPath, maxConnections, queueTimeoutMs, executionTimeoutMs)
(1)_pool_creation_sites.context: Custom DuckDB subprocess-based pool. Not a pg Pool, but the same pattern — limited concurrent connections (default 4), queuing, timeout. Used for read-only analytical queries.

config.note: No poolSize, idleTimeout, max, min, or connectionTimeoutMillis passed. All pg.Pool defaults apply (default max=10, idleTimeoutMillis=10000, connectionTimeoutMillis=10000, etc.)
poolSize is declared in ConfigSchema (line 7) but NEVER wired into the Pool constructor in db.pg.ts. It's a dead config option — the init() function ignores it.

(2)_pool_config_options.finding: DEAD CONFIG: OPENCODE_DATABASE_POOL_SIZE (env/default 10) is declared in database-config.ts line 7 but never passed to new Pool() in db.pg.ts line 18. Pool always uses pg's default max=10.

(3)_connection_lifecycle.description: Pool created but NOT connected at init time. pg.Pool is lazy — it establishes connections on first query. PGlite starts the WASM process eagerly.

Calls $client.close() on the Drizzle-wrapped client. For PGlite this closes the WASM backend. For pg Pool this calls pool.close() which waits for idle clients to drain then closes the pool. Note: $client on a NodePgDatabase is the Pool itself — pool.close() is correct. But this is typed as 'any' cast — no compile-time safety.

Uses .end() on the underlying Pool. Note: migrate.ts calls .end(), db.ts calls .close(). For pg.Pool, both exist: .end() drains + closes, .close() is an alias. For PGlite, .close() exists but .end() does not — hence the typeof check.
Custom pool close — sets closed flag, rejects queued requests, does NOT kill active subprocesses.

(3)_connection_lifecycle.code:
function makePgAdapter(url: string, ssl: boolean): Interface {
  const client = initPg(url, ssl)
(3)_connection_lifecycle.description: The Effect Service adapter creates the client but has NO close/shutdown mechanism. No Layer.finalizer, no Scope management. If the PgAdapter layer is replaced or the app shuts down, the Pool is never explicitly closed via the Effect layer.

(4)_error_paths.finding: Query errors are ALWAYS marked isRetryable: false — no retry on normal query failures, even if they're transient (network blip, pool timeout). Only transaction serialization errors get retried.

(4)_error_paths.scenario: PG unavailable at init (connection string level)
(4)_error_paths.code:
const pool = new Pool({ connectionString, ssl })
return drizzlePg({ client: pool })
(4)_error_paths.finding: NO error handling at pool creation. pg.Pool constructor doesn't throw on bad connection strings (it's lazy). Errors surface on first query, but there's no connection validation at init. PGlite will throw synchronously if dataDir is invalid.

Only PG error code 40001 (serialization_failure) is considered retryable. No handling for 40P01 (deadlock_detected), 08000 (connection exception), 08001 (sqlclient_unable_to_establish_sqlconnection), or any other transient error.

(4)_error_paths.code:
const PG_CONNECTION_STRING_RE = /(?:postgres(?:ql)?:\/\/)[^\s]*/gi
function sanitizePgError(cause: unknown): object { ... }
(4)_error_paths.finding: Redacts connection strings from error messages. Extracts message, code, hint, detail fields. Drops everything else. Good security practice.

(5)_retry_degradation_logic.description: Exponential backoff retry for serialization failures (code 40001). Starts at 100ms, doubles each attempt, up to 3 retries. Only applies to transactions, not single queries. No jitter.

(5)_retry_degradation_logic.code: catch: (cause) => new DatabaseError({ message: "Query failed", cause: sanitizePgError(cause), isRetryable: false })
(5)_retry_degradation_logic.description: isRetryable is hard-coded to false. Queries that hit connection errors, pool timeouts, or any non-serialization failure are NOT retried anywhere.

(5)_retry_degradation_logic.finding: There is NO automatic fallback from PG to SQLite at runtime. The defaultLayer in adapter.ts (line 368-375) chooses at Layer construction time based on whether OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL is set. If PG is configured but unreachable at query time, the application crashes with DatabaseError — no circuit breaker, no health check, no fallback.

(5)_retry_degradation_logic.description: DuckDB pool has queue timeout (30s default). PG pool has no equivalent — it relies on pg.Pool's internal connectionTimeoutMillis (default 10s) which is unconfigured.

(6)_dollar_client_access_patterns.context: Migrate accesses $client to call .end() on the underlying pool. For PGlite, $client is the PGlite instance (has exec(), close()). For node-postgres, $client is the Pool instance (has end(), close()). Uses typeof check to distinguish.
applyMigrations uses $client to get the raw client for duck-type checking (typeof client.exec === 'function' → PGlite path vs node-postgres path).
db.ts close() calls $client.close() on the singleton. This assumes $client has a .close() method. For PGlite it does. For pg.Pool, close() exists as alias for end().
Inside makeSQLiteAdapter, there's a reference to $client — but this is the SQLite adapter path, not PG. It uses a duck-type fallback pattern.

importers_of_#db: packages/opencode/src/storage/db.ts (line 11) — uses init() for singleton client
packages/opencode/src/storage/adapter.ts (line 4) — uses init() for PgAdapter layer

singleton_flow.db.ts → init() (from #db): Creates singleton pg client at module level (line 54). Exposed via Client() getter. close() calls $client.close() then resets singleton.

effect_layer_flow.adapter.ts → initPg (from #db): makePgAdapter calls initPg() inside Effect.sync. Pool created at layer construction time, not at app startup. No Layer.finalizer for pool cleanup.

migration_flow.migrate.ts → initPg + applyMigrations (f: Creates PG client, applies migrations, then closes underlying client via $client.end(). This is the ONLY path that consistently closes the pool.

gaps_and_risks.finding: Pool size config is dead code
gaps_and_risks.detail: OPENCODE_DATABASE_POOL_SIZE declared in database-config.ts line 7, default 10, but never passed to new Pool() in db.pg.ts. pg.Pool defaults to 10 anyway by coincidence, but customizing it has no effect.

PgAdapter (adapter.ts line 273) creates a Layer.effect with no finalizer. If the layer is replaced or the app shuts down via Effect scope, the underlying pg.Pool is never drained/closed. Only migrate.ts (line 60) and db.ts (line 71) close the pool, and they use different methods (.end() vs .close()).

gaps_and_risks.finding: No retry on transient query errors
gaps_and_risks.detail: Single-query errors are always isRetryable: false (adapter.ts line 199). Network blips, pool timeouts, and deadlocks (code 40P01) will fail immediately. Only serialization failures (40001) on transactions get exponential backoff.

migrate.ts calls .end() on $client, db.ts calls .close(). Both work for pg.Pool (close is an alias for end) but PGlite has only .close(). The typeof check at migrate.ts:59 handles this, but db.ts:71 does not guard.

gaps_and_risks.finding: pg.Pool configured with no pool-level timeouts
gaps_and_risks.detail: new Pool({ connectionString, ssl }) does not set connectionTimeoutMillis, idleTimeoutMillis, max, or any pool sizing. pg defaults: 10 connections, 10s idle timeout, 10s connection timeout. All are implicit — none are explicit or configurable from env vars except via the dead poolSize config.
Every call to $client uses (client as any).$client. This bypasses type safety. If the Drizzle API changes, these will silently break at runtime.
No startup validation that PG is reachable, no pgBouncer mode, no statement timeout configuration, no health-check ping endpoint. Errors surface on first actual query.

recommended_actions: Wire OPENCODE_DATABASE_POOL_SIZE into new Pool() constructor in db.pg.ts
Add Layer.finalizer to PgAdapter that calls pool.end() on layer shutdown
Expand retryable error codes to include 40P01 (deadlock_detected), 08000/08001 (connection failures)
Add configurable connectionTimeoutMillis and idleTimeoutMillis to Pool constructor via env vars
Add a startup health check that validates PG connectivity before declaring the app ready
Align close/end method usage — prefer .end() consistently and check for its existence
